title: cercocarpus montanus var. paucidentatus
intro: Cercocarpus montanus var. paucidentatus, also known as the little-leaf mountain mahogany, is a variety of shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rhamnaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cercocarpus montanus var. paucidentatus are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and applications.
